creator: pfgshield-freebsd@yahoo.com
text:
--- Stefan Walter <stefan@FreeBSD.org> escribió:

> pfgshield-freebsd@yahoo.com, 06.10.07, 19:28h CEST:
> 
> > --- Stefan Walter <stefan@FreeBSD.org> escribió:
> > 
> > ...
> > > 
> > > There is only one port making use of math/umfpack: math/freemat. And with
> > > that one, it's only used if it's already installed.
> > > 
> > 
> > According to freshports you have to "fix" the following ports:
> > 
> > math/arpack++
> > math/freefem++
> > math/octave-devel
> > math/petsc
> > science/mbdyn
> > 
...
> What do you mean when you write "fix"? The listed ports use
> math/suitesparse. Do you intend to change them to use math/umfpack
> instead?
> 

Ooops no. I ran a search on freshports "Build/Lib/run containing umfpack" and
those came out:

http://www.freshports.org/search.php?stype=depends_all&method=match&query=umfpack&num=100&orderby=category&orderbyupdown=asc&search=Search


but I checked and you are right. Hmm.. yes, I guess we can let umfpack go.

    Pedro.
